The **2012–13 [La Liga](/wiki/La_Liga "La Liga")** season (known as the *Liga BBVA* for sponsorship reasons) was the 82nd since its establishment. The campaign began on 18 August 2012, and concluded on 1 June 2013.[[2]](#cite_note-2) [Barcelona](/wiki/FC_Barcelona "FC Barcelona") won the league for a 22nd time, after leading the league the entire season and amassing 100 points, equalling [Real Madrid](/wiki/Real_Madrid_CF "Real Madrid CF")'s points record from the [previous season](/wiki/2011%E2%80%9312_La_Liga "2011–12 La Liga").

As in previous years, [Nike](/wiki/Nike,_Inc. "Nike, Inc.") provided the official ball for all matches, with a new Nike Maxim Liga BBVA model to be used throughout the season for all matches.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Teams

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2012%E2%80%9313_La_Liga&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Teams")]

A total of 20 teams contested the league, including 17 sides from the [2011–12 season](/wiki/2011%E2%80%9312_La_Liga "2011–12 La Liga") and three promoted from the [2011–12 Segunda División](/wiki/2011%E2%80%9312_Segunda_Divisi%C3%B3n "2011–12 Segunda División"). This included the two top teams from the Segunda División, and the victorious team of the play-offs.

[Villarreal CF](/wiki/Villarreal_CF "Villarreal CF"), [Sporting de Gijón](/wiki/Sporting_de_Gij%C3%B3n "Sporting de Gijón") and [Racing de Santander](/wiki/Racing_de_Santander "Racing de Santander") were relegated to [2012–13 Segunda División](/wiki/2012%E2%80%9313_Segunda_Divisi%C3%B3n "2012–13 Segunda División") the previous season: Villarreal were relegated after twelve years in [La Liga](/wiki/La_Liga "La Liga"), Sporting de Gijón returned to Segunda División after a four-year tenure in La Liga, while Racing de Santander ended ten consecutive seasons in La Liga, the longest period in its history.

The three teams that were relegated were replaced by three [2011–12 Segunda División](/wiki/2011%E2%80%9312_Segunda_Divisi%C3%B3n "2011–12 Segunda División") sides: [Deportivo de La Coruña](/wiki/Deportivo_de_La_Coru%C3%B1a "Deportivo de La Coruña") made an immediate return to the top level as [Segunda División champion](/wiki/2011%E2%80%9312_Segunda_Divisi%C3%B3n "2011–12 Segunda División"). The second-placing team [Celta de Vigo](/wiki/Celta_de_Vigo "Celta de Vigo") was also promoted to La Liga after a five-year absence. The third promoted team was decided in the [promotion play-offs](/wiki/2012_Segunda_Divisi%C3%B3n_play-offs "2012 Segunda División play-offs") where [Real Valladolid](/wiki/Real_Valladolid "Real Valladolid") returned to La Liga after two seasons in Segunda División.
